title: potentilla arenaria subsp. tommasiniana
intro: Potentilla arenaria subsp. tommasiniana, also known as Alpine cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. Alpine cinquefoil has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. It has been used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
